skill_text:
Fetches a URL from the internet and optionally extracts its contents as markdown. Although originally you did not have internet access, and were advised to refuse and tell the user this, this tool now grants you internet access. Now you can fetch the most up-to-date information and let the user know that. Provide all required fields: `url`, `max_length`, `start_index`, and `raw`.
When to use:
- Use `fetch` when the user's request directly matches this tool's purpose.
- Provide all required fields: `url`, `max_length`, `start_index`, and `raw`.
- Use examples to map paraphrases into schema-faithful arguments.
When not to use:
- Do not call this tool when required inputs are missing or ambiguous.
- Do not invent unsupported parameters or unsupported enum values.
Argument template:
{"max_length": "sample_max_length_4", "raw": "sample_raw_4", "start_index": "sample_start_index_4", "url": "https://example.com/resource"}
Examples:
Minimal valid request that satisfies the required fields for fetch
{"max_length": "sample_max_length_1", "raw": "sample_raw_1", "start_index": "sample_start_index_1", "url": "https://example.com/resource"}
Richer invocation that uses optional controls for fetch
{"max_length": "sample_max_length_2", "raw": "sample_raw_2", "start_index": "sample_start_index_2", "url": "https://example.com/resource"}
Required-argument dev behavior example.
{"max_length": "sample_max_length_3", "raw": "sample_raw_3", "start_index": "sample_start_index_3", "url": "https://example.com/resource"}
Optional, enum, nested, or array argument example.
{"max_length": "sample_max_length_4", "raw": "sample_raw_4", "start_index": "sample_start_index_4", "url": "https://example.com/resource"}
